title: astragalus arenarius
intro: Astragalus arenarius, also known as sand smilax or Mongolian milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family, characterized by its small, white or pinkish flowers and edible seeds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. Astragalus arenarius has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
